subject: PATIENT
heading: Brief History of Physical Illness__page_no__5
text: brief history of physical illness 81-year-old man presents with agitation. patient was apparently just discharged from a rehab facility this morning. he was brought home from the rehab facility by ambulance. unable to be cared for at home due to his agitation and altered mental status. the patient has reportedly some history of dementia. son is concerned that he has not been getting his depakote, seroquel, rasper down at the facility where he has been staying. son reports that he has also been losing weight and not eating and drinking, refusing medications at the facility. here in the emergency department, he is not directable, is unable to answer any questions and is attempting to ambulate unsafely around the room. his workup shows findings of a mild leukocytosis with left shift. the reason for this is not completely clear. urine shows some red cells although not otherwise suggestive of infection. noted to have ketones. iv fluids were initiated. his depakote levels on measurably low. ct head was obtained and shows no acute findings. x-ray shows findings of possible pneumonia although he has had no associated cardiopulmonary symptoms and is not coughing here in the ed. the patient is not hypoxic with clear lungs and has no abdominal tenderness. he has noted to be going in and out of atrial fibrillation with a rapid ventricular response. [1] patient is unable to communicate or give any meaningful history and not following the commands well. [1]

subject: PATIENT
heading: Discharge Documentation__page_no__6
text: discharge documentation [encounter_date] patient evaluated bedside as above this is a 81-year-old gentleman with progressive dementia my understanding and discussing with the patient's son [patient_name] that the patient has been recent hospitalization transferred to skilled facility and the patient was subsequently discharged home in review of the chest x-ray the patient is having aspiration difficulties. patient was also evaluated by speech therapy and nursing who are reporting that the patient is having choking and difficulty with probable aspiration. evaluation at bedside the patient does seem to have upper respiratory airway congestion. therefore the patient is to be kept n.p.o. i discussed with the patient's son [patient_name] who has power of attorney the current modalities of nutritional intake that the patient will require. he is indicated the patient had been on hospice with some confusion. but indicating the patient will not be on hospice anymore and wants to go forward with peg and nutritional support. the patient will be continued on antibiotic therapy to treat the underlying aspiration pneumonia we will consult gastroenterology for peg placement [patient_name] indicates he will be coming to the hospital to sign documentation for approval of peg placement noted the patient has an underlying history of progressive dementia. 1. aspiration pneumonia continue the patient on antibiotic therapy with clindamycin and rocephin 2. progressive dementia chronic 3. debility 4 chronic atrial fibrillation current time we will hold off of anv anticoaqulation due to the debility of the patient and the need for procedures. 5. i discussed with [patient_name] at length the plan of treatment and decision making he and his brother will be presenting today we will move forward with nutritional support via peg tube benefits the family has requested. 6. nutritional support via peg placement will consult gastroenterology [1]

text: discharge documentation [encounter_date] patient evaluated bedside as above this is a 81-year-old gentleman with progressive dementia my understanding and discussing with the patient's son [patient_name] that the patient has been recent hospitalization transferred to skilled facility and the patient was subsequently discharged home in review of the chest x-ray the patient is having aspiration difficulties. patient was also evaluated by speech therapy and nursing who are reporting that the patient is having choking and difficulty with probable aspiration. evaluation at bedside the patient does seem to have upper respiratory airway congestion. therefore the patient is to be kept n.p.o. i discussed with the patient's son [patient_name] who has power of attorney the current modalities of nutritional intake that the patient will require. he is indicated the patient had been on hospice with some confusion. but indicating the patient will not be on hospice anymore and wants to go forward with peg and nutritional support. the patient will be continued on antibiotic therapy to treat the underlying aspiration pneumonia we will consult gastroenterology for peg placement [patient_name] indicates he will be coming to the hospital to sign documentation for approval of peg placement noted the patient has an underlying history of progressive dementia. 1. aspiration pneumonia continue the patient on antibiotic therapy with clindamycin and rocephin 2. progressive dementia chronic 3. debility 4 chronic atrial fibrillation current time we will hold off of anv anticoaqulation due to the debility of the patient and the need for procedures. 5. i discussed with [patient_name] at length the plan of treatment and decision making he and his brother will be presenting today we will move forward with nutritional support via peg tube benefits the family has requested. 6. nutritional support via peg placement will consult gastroenterology [1]
